finding: Closing sibling tasks against one intentional shared delivery commit (`--historical-delivery`) fails without `--waiver-out-of-scope-delivery`, and dry-run/write messaging mixes `MIXED_DELIVERY_COMMIT`, `OUT_OF_SCOPE_WAIVER_REQUIRED`, and `DELIVERABLE_DIFF_REQUIRED` until the waiver is present.
expectedBehavior: When the operator already passes `--historical-delivery` for co-delivered siblings owned by the same actor batch, ATM should offer a first-class multi-task delivery acknowledgment (or auto-suggest the exact waiver command with the sibling task list) instead of looking like a failed delivery.
evidenceOrRepro: Dogfood 2026-07-14: TASK-AAO-0197..0200 against delivery `c7e3e81a` after TASK-AAO-0196 scope-amended shared commit; close succeeded only after `--waiver-out-of-scope-delivery --reason`.
followUp: Fixed in `packages/cli/src/commands/taskflow/write-readiness.ts` and `historical-close-preflight.ts`: shared `--historical-delivery` close now promotes the exact waiver command as the primary blocker, demotes missing-delivery codes, and surfaces a `multiTaskCloseRecipe` sibling hint. Validation: `node --strip-types packages/cli/src/commands/taskflow/__tests__/mixed-delivery-close-ux.spec.ts`.
